name: Diabetic Chocolate Balls
recipeInstructions:
1. Sprinkle sweet granulated sugar substitute as needed.
2. Cream together margarine and sugar until light and fluffy.
3. Add vanilla and sugar substitute to the creamed mixture. Beat at medium speed for 30 seconds.
4. Stir together flour, cocoa, and salt in a separate bowl. Add this mixture to the creamed mixture and mix at low speed for about 1 minute or until blended.
5. Add nuts and raisins to the dough. Mix lightly.
6. Shape into balls using approximately one tablespoon of dough per ball.
7. Place balls on a cookie sheet lined with aluminum foil or sprayed with PAN spray.
8. Bake at 325°F (165°C) for 20-25 minutes, or until slightly firm.
9. Remove from the oven and let cool slightly.
10. Roll the cooled balls in additional sweet granulated sugar substitute.
11. Allow to cool to room temperature before serving. Serve two balls per serving.
Variation: Chocolate Mint Balls
Add 1/2 teaspoon peppermint flavoring along with 1 teaspoon vanilla instead of the 2 teaspoons vanilla.
